channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Analysis of Trump’s messaging and strategy as Iran ceasefire deadline approaches
item.description: This Guardian Australia podcast episode examines Donald Trump’s behaviour and messaging as a ceasefire deadline involving Iran approaches, focusing on the uncertainty surrounding his strategic intent....

item.title: Labatt’s Commitment to Sustainability: A Collaborative Effort Across its Operations
item.description: Labatt Breweries of Canada has long been a leader in environmental sustainability, having conducted its first comprehensive environmental policy review over 50 years ago. The company has been investin...

item.title: Parliament Honours the Legacy of Actress and Cultural Pioneer Cynthia Shange
item.description: South Africa’s Parliament has formally paid tribute to the late Cynthia Shange, recognising her as a trailblazing actress and a powerful cultural figure who helped shape the country’s artistic and soc...

item.title: Sindh CM Reviews Progress: Rs146.77m Disbursed to 3,976 Vehicles in Fuel Subsidy Scheme
item.description: Chief Minister of Sindh Syed Murad Ali Shah chaired a meeting at CM House to review the fuel subsidy scheme for the transport sector, launched in response to recent fuel price hikes. So far, 3,976 veh...

item.title: Pre-surgery immunotherapy prevents bowel cancer recurrence in clinical trial
item.description: A pioneering clinical trial conducted by University College London (UCL) and University College London Hospitals (UCLH) has delivered promising results for patients with certain types of bowel cancer....

item.title: BJP Leader Visits Lenskart Mumbai Store to Support Employees Wearing Religious Symbols
item.description: In Mumbai, a controversy arose over Lenskart’s internal dress code, prompting BJP minority morcha leader Nazia Elahi Khan to visit one of its stores. Allegations had surfaced that Hindu staff were dis...

item.title: DOJ Plans to Use UN Anti-Corruption Treaty to Repatriate Zaldy Co from Abroad
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has announced its intention to invoke the United Nations Convention against Corruption (UNCAC) to facilitate the return of former public official Zaldy Co to the Philip...

item.title: US-Iran Talks: Islamabad Remains Key as Diplomatic Efforts Continue
item.description: The situation around the US-Iran conflict remains tense, with President Trump repeatedly adjusting the timeline for ending the war, shifting from “days” to “weeks” and now to “soon.” Islamabad is once...

item.title: TMC's Poster Campaign Dominates Over BJP in Bengal's Election Battle
item.description: As West Bengal prepares for its upcoming assembly elections, the Trinamool Congress (TMC) appears to have taken a clear lead over the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) in one key area – the poster campaign...

item.title: Gachagua Plans London Visit to Prepare for 2027 Elections and Strengthen DCP
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has announced plans for a brief trip to London, citing the need to organise resources and prepare for political engagements ahead of the 2027 elections. Speaki...

item.title: The Potential Impact of AI-Generated Personas on Democratic Processes
item.description: Researchers from the University of British Columbia warn of an emerging threat to democratic systems: highly realistic AI-generated personas that can influence public opinion online. Unlike traditiona...

item.title: Jon Stewart Reacts to Trump's Unusual Signature During Psychedelic Drug Executive Order Signing

item.title: Family mourns Johannesburg man killed in Emmarentia road rage shooting
item.description: Faisal Ul Rehman, 48, tragically lost his life in a road rage incident on Barry Hertzog Avenue, Emmarentia, Johannesburg, over the past weekend. The altercation began as a minor fender-bender and esca...

item.title: Practical ways Nigerian students can earn money while waiting for university admission
item.description: For many Nigerian students wey don finish exams and dey wait for JAMB or university admission, time fit drag and money no dey. This article show 10 practical ways to earn small small money without deg...

item.title: Australian sharemarket edges lower as energy and gold stocks decline amid US-Iran tensions
item.description: The Australian sharemarket finished almost flat on Tuesday, with the S&P/ASX 200 closing down just 3.9 points at 8949.40. Investors are watching developments in US-Iran peace talks after escalating te...

item.title: Stars Gather for The Devil Wears Prada 2 Premiere in New York
item.description: Anne Hathaway graced the red carpet in New York City for the premiere of 'The Devil Wears Prada 2', looking radiant in a crimson strapless Louis Vuitton gown with a pleated skirt. She reunited with co...

item.title: Kenya Transport Fare Hikes Under Scrutiny Amid Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: The National Taxpayers Association (NTA) has expressed concern over rising public transport fares, cautioning that some operators may be unfairly taking advantage of recent fuel price increases. CEO P...

item.title: Trump grants pardon to nursing home owner amid unresolved family lawsuit
item.description: In a controversial move, former President Donald Trump issued a pardon to Joseph Schwartz, the owner of a nursing home in the United States, who was facing a nearly $19 million judgment stemming from ...

item.title: Court to hear final evidence on alleged police abuse in Lusikisiki mass murder case
item.description: The Mthatha High Court in the Eastern Cape is preparing to hear the final testimony in a trial within a trial linked to the Lusikisiki mass murder case. This legal process is focused on determining wh...

item.title: The decline of tabloid newspapers in the digital era
item.description: The article examines the ongoing decline of traditional tabloid newspapers, arguing that their long-term survival—whether in print or digital form—is increasingly unlikely. Triggered by a recent Finan...
